nodes.en: Hands-on skills and trades that build, maintain, and repair the physical fabric of daily life — structures, machines, circuits, pipes, wood, metal, gardens, food, and cloth. Spans construction, mechanics, electronics, plumbing, carpentry, welding, gardening, cooking, sewing, and automotive work; distinguished from engineering by its basis in manual craft and working practice over design theory.
nodes.cs: Praktické dovednosti a řemesla, jež budují, udržují a opravují hmotné zázemí každodenního života — stavby, stroje, obvody, potrubí, dřevo, kov, zahrady, jídlo i textil. Zahrnují stavebnictví, mechaniku, elektroniku, instalatérství, tesařství, svařování, zahradničení, vaření, šití a automechaniku; od inženýrství se liší tím, že vycházejí z ruční zručnosti a pracovní praxe, nikoli z teorie návrhu.
nodes.brief:
Practical skills and trades are the hands-on disciplines that build, maintain, and repair the physical fabric of daily life. Their knowledge is embodied and procedural — acquired through apprenticeship and practice, verified by whether the joint holds, the circuit carries, and the engine runs — and it forms the working counterpart to the design knowledge of engineering.

[[07.01]] Construction erects and finishes buildings; [[07.05]] Carpentry and [[07.06]] Welding work wood and metal into structure; [[07.02]] Mechanics and [[07.10]] Automotive keep machines and vehicles running; [[07.03]] Electronics assembles and repairs circuits and devices; [[07.04]] Plumbing installs and maintains water and waste systems; and [[07.07]] Gardening, [[07.08]] Cooking, and [[07.09]] Sewing sustain the household economy of food and clothing.

The trades execute and maintain what [[03]] Applied Sciences & Technology designs, shade into [[06]] Arts & Creative Expression where craft rises to aesthetic ambition, and supply the manual competence on which [[08]] Survival & Emergency Preparedness and [[12]] Post-Disaster Rebuilding depend when specialized infrastructure is unavailable.
